title: Music Department Annual Update 20-21 SY (Part 1)
text:
College of the Siskiyous Music Department Annual Update
Prepared: January 24, 2021 – Ron Slabbinck, Music Faculty
Reviewed/Edited: January 25, 2021 – David Blink, Music Faculty
1. Describe annual progress / changes / events in the program.
A. Most significant challenge – COVID-19
● Reduction in class offerings (ensembles) and reduction in student enrollment
● Loss of live, in person instruction, rehearsal, and performance
● Dealing with technology and music equipment needs of instructors and students
B. Most significant growth/learning – COVID-19
● Developed non-traditional means for music instruction (traditionally face-to-face
adapted to remote)
● More courses now able to be taught remotely
● New pedagogy and instruction techniques discovered in remote instruction can be
applied to traditional face-to-face instruction
● Focusing more on student ‘wellbeing’
2. Describe progress on assessing SLOs and PLOs for the program.
● Transition from Curricunet to eLumen in 2018 resulted in an auto-update of all
courses in Fall 2018. However, this is not the case. Therefore, MUS courses will
be updated and made current early in 2021.
● This Curricunet to eLumen transfer has also revealed other issues, as some SLOs
did not transfer correctly, and some did not transfer at all. Therefore, as Courses
are updated, SLOs will be examined against archived CORs to ensure that all
SLOs are present. Then, any SLOs that are outdated or need updating can be
addressed.
● The degree course sequence/requirement modifications indicated in last year’s
comprehensive Program Review will be submitted to the Curriculum Committee
early in 2021. COVID-19 paused this action, as focus and attention was
necessarily directed at modifying instruction to be delivered remotely. Once
complete, the new degree course sequence will ensure students a path to
graduation with either a Music Degree for Transfer, or a local AA in Music in not
more than two full academic school years.

-- 1 of 4 --

title: Music Department Annual Update 20-21 SY (Part 2)
text:
3. Discuss any support or obstacles encountered by the program.
Obstacles
● COVID-19
○ Listed Above
● Shrinking Local H.S. music programs
○ COVID
○ budget constraints
○ diminishing student population
● Decreased College Budget
○ Multiple factors contribute to this issue
● Still recovering from zeroed out CQIPs
○ Hope to resolve for next budget cycle
Support
● Dean and Vice President of Academic Affairs
○ Understand value of music and the arts
○ Supportive of creative scheduling/course offerings
● New Construction Project
○ Students feel valued
○ Facility will meet function and technology needs
○ Assist in future recruiting
● Facilities
○ Had a great working relationship trying to invent a new way for college gear to be
checked out to students who did not have the equipment due to the loss of on
campus instruction
● Counseling and Advising
○ Continued a good working relationship with members of the counseling and
advising team assisting with students struggling keeping their academics up
during the pandemic. Also, great continued support on advising current students
as they gear up for their next educational goal (transfering to a 4-year institution.
● Additional Adjunct Faculty member
○ More flexibility in course offerings and times
○ Functions as both Adjunct Instructor and EIA for ensembles and music majors
4. Describe program budgetary needs or implications. (Submit a CQIP if you are requesting a
budget allocation that falls outside of regular yearly budget allocations).

-- 2 of 4 --

The following budget items are carried over from previous Annual updates and Program
Reviews. CQIPs will be submitted to resolve all of the line items that had been zeroed out.
● 2112-1110 Faculty - Full Time
○ A full-time piano instructor/staff accompanist position is essential to the growth
and development of the music and theatre departments. Currently, the new
adjunct instructor serves this purpose.
● 2112-2126 Class-Overtime
○ For all music performances (on and off campus), Tech and stage support requires
additional staff
○ Twelve Concerts/year
○ Six Dress Rehearsals/year
○ Growth - Add 2-4 additional Concerts
● 2112-2211 Instr Aide (Instr, Reg)
○ Continued need to grow as student enrollment in music courses grows
● 2112-2321 Student Help (Noninstr) - District
○ Create inventory of music and instruments
○ Assist with clerical/photocopying
○ Assist with set-up for concerts and performances
○ Counselors for Summer Jazz/Showchoir Camp
○ Help organize, categorize, and purge music department materials for construction
project
● 2112-2411 Instructional Aide - Direct
○ Applied Music Instructors / Accompanists
○ Increased number of ensembles and music majors require more hours for
accompanists and EIAs
● 2112-4130 Instructional Supplies - Other
○ Music Scores needed for the following ensembles (anticipated to return in 21-22
SY)
■ Concert Band
■ Jazz Band
■ Orchestra
■ Chamber Ensembles
■ Concert Choir (Weed)
■ Concert Choir (Yreka)
■ Chamber Choir
■ Vocal Jazz Ensemble
■ Beginning Vocal Jazz Ensemble
■ Summer Jazz/Showchoir Camp
● 2112-4140 A.V. Supplies (Instr)
○ Instructor Station Technology in T1, T2, T3
